data.content:
### Install E2B CLI using NPM

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/packages/cli/README.md

Installs the E2B CLI tool globally on your system using the Node Package Manager (NPM). This method is suitable for users who have Node.js and NPM installed.

```bash
npm install -g @e2b/cli
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI with NPM

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/cli/page.mdx

Installs the E2B CLI globally using NPM. This method is suitable for users who have Node.js and NPM installed. It makes the E2B CLI commands accessible from any directory in your terminal.

```bash
npm i -g @e2b/cli
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI Beta Version with NPM

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/cli/page.mdx

Installs the beta version of the E2B CLI globally using NPM. This allows you to test the latest features and improvements before they are released in a stable version. Ensure you have Node.js and NPM installed.

```bash
npm i -g @e2b/cli@beta
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node.js Packages at Runtime (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs Node.js packages like 'cowsay' during the sandbox runtime using `sbx.commands.run`. It then shows how to use the installed package within the sandbox.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run('npm install cowsay') // This will install the cowsay package
sbx.runCode(`
  const cowsay = require('cowsay')
  console.log(cowsay.say({ text: 'Hello, world!' }))
`, { language: 'javascript' })
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node.js Packages at Runtime (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs Node.js packages like 'cowsay' during the sandbox runtime using `sbx.commands.run`. It then demonstrates using the installed package within the sandbox.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("npm install cowsay") # This will install the cowsay package
sbx.run_code("""
  import { say } from 'cowsay'
  console.log(say('Hello, world!'))
""", language="javascript")
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B SDK (npm and pip)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/page.mdx

Installs the necessary E2B SDK packages for code execution and environment variable loading using npm for JavaScript/TypeScript and pip for Python.

```js
npm i @e2b/code-interpreter dotenv
```

```python
pip install e2b-code-interpreter python-dotenv
```

--------------------------------

### Install Python Packages at Runtime (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs Python packages like 'cowsay' during the sandbox runtime using the `sbx.commands.run` method. It then demonstrates using the installed package.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run('pip install cowsay') // This will install the cowsay package
sbx.runCode(`
  import cowsay
  cowsay.cow("Hello, world!")
`)
```

--------------------------------

### Install Debian Packages at Runtime (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs system packages like 'curl' and 'git' using APT on Debian-based sandboxes via `sbx.commands.run`. This allows for broader system utility installations.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create()
await sbx.commands.run('apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl git')
```

--------------------------------

### Install Debian Packages at Runtime (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs system packages like 'curl' and 'git' using APT on Debian-based sandboxes via `sbx.commands.run`. This enables the installation of various system utilities.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl git")
```

--------------------------------

### Install Python Packages at Runtime (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs Python packages like 'cowsay' during the sandbox runtime using the `sbx.commands.run` method. It then demonstrates using the installed package.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("pip install cowsay") # This will install the cowsay package
sbx.run_code("""
  import cowsay
  cowsay.cow("Hello, world!")
""")
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B Code Interpreter SDK (npm)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/packages/js-sdk/README.md

Installs the E2B code interpreter SDK for JavaScript and Node.js environments using npm. This is the first step to interact with E2B sandboxes programmatically.

```bash
npm i @e2b/code-interpreter
```

--------------------------------

### Run TypeScript code with Axios in E2B

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/code-interpreting/supported-languages/javascript/page.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to run TypeScript code that utilizes the `axios` library to fetch data from an API. It shows the process of creating a sandbox, installing external packages like `axios` using `npm install`, and then executing the TypeScript code. The example includes handling of top-level await and ESM-style imports.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from "@e2b/code-interpreter";

// Create a new sandbox
const sbx = await Sandbox.create();

// Install the axios package
await sbx.commands.run("npm install axios");

// Run the code
const execution = await sbx.runCode(`
  import axios from "axios";

  const url: string = "https://api.github.com/status";
  const response = await axios.get(url);
  response.data;
`,
  { language: "ts" }
);

console.log(execution);
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

# Create a new sandbox
sbx = Sandbox.create()

# Install the axios package
sbx.commands.run("npm install axios")

#  Run the code
execution = sbx.run_code("""
  import axios from "axios";

  const url: string = "https://api.github.com/status";
  const response = await axios.get(url);
  response.data;
""",
    language="ts",
)

print(execution)
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Packages in e2b.Dockerfile

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Defines custom packages to be installed within a sandbox template by editing the `e2b.Dockerfile`. It uses a base image and `RUN` commands for package installation.

```dockerfile
FROM e2bdev/code-interpreter:latest

RUN pip install cowsay
RUN npm install cowsay
```

--------------------------------

### Login to E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Authenticates the E2B CLI with your E2B account. This command is necessary before you can create or manage custom sandbox templates.

```bash
e2b auth login
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI with Homebrew (macOS)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/cli/page.mdx

Installs the E2B CLI using the Homebrew package manager. This is a straightforward installation for macOS users. It ensures you have the latest stable version of the CLI available in your terminal.

```bash
brew install e2b
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize E2B Sandbox Template

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Initializes a new sandbox template configuration. This command sets up the necessary files and structure for defining a custom sandbox environment.

```bash
e2b template init
```

--------------------------------

### Set Global Environment Variables on Sandbox Creation (JS/Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/environment-variables/page.mdx

This code illustrates how to set global environment variables when creating a new E2B sandbox. These variables will be available for all subsequent operations within that sandbox. The `envs` option is passed to the `Sandbox.create()` method.

```js
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create({
  envs: { 
    MY_VAR: 'my_value',
  },
})
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox.create(
  envs={
    'MY_VAR': 'my_value',
  },
)
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B Code Interpreter SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/README.md

Installs the E2B Code Interpreter SDK for JavaScript/TypeScript via npm and for Python via pip. This is the first step to using the E2B service.

```bash
npm i @e2b/code-interpreter
```

```bash
pip install e2b-code-interpreter
```

--------------------------------

### Start Custom Sandbox with Template ID (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Starts a new sandbox instance using a previously built custom template. The template ID is passed to the `Sandbox.create` method in JavaScript.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create({
  template: 'YOUR_TEMPLATE_ID',
})
```

--------------------------------

### Build E2B Sandbox Template

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Builds a custom sandbox template from the Dockerfile. This process converts the Docker image into a runnable sandbox VM, which can take a significant amount of time.

```bash
e2b template build -c "/root/.jupyter/start-up.sh"
```

--------------------------------

### Install gcsfuse for Google Cloud Storage in Dockerfile

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/connect-bucket/page.mdx

This Dockerfile installs the gcsfuse package, which allows mounting Google Cloud Storage buckets as a file system within a Docker container. It requires updating package lists, installing dependencies, adding the GCS FUSE repository, and then installing gcsfuse. Ensure the correct repository is added based on the Ubuntu release.

```docker
FROM e2bdev/code-interpreter:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gnupg lsb-release wget

RUN lsb_release -c -s > /tmp/lsb_release
RUN GCSFUSE_REPO=$(cat /tmp/lsb_release) && echo "deb https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt gcsfuse-$GCSFUSE_REPO main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/gcsfuse.list
RUN wget -O - https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | apt-key add -

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gcsfuse

```

--------------------------------

### Set Environment Variables for Command Execution (JS/Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/environment-variables/page.mdx

This snippet demonstrates setting environment variables for a specific command execution within an E2B sandbox. Similar to running code, these variables are temporary and override global settings for the command's scope. The `envs` option is passed to the `sandbox.commands.run()` method.

```js
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
sandbox.commands.run('echo $MY_VAR', {
  envs: { 
    MY_VAR: '123',
  },
})
```

```python
sandbox = Sandbox.create()
sandbox.commands.run(
    'echo $MY_VAR',
    envs={
        'MY_VAR': '123'
    }
)
```

--------------------------------

### Set Environment Variables for Code Execution (JS/Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/environment-variables/page.mdx

This example shows how to set environment variables specifically for a single code execution call within an E2B sandbox. These variables override any global variables with the same name for the duration of that specific command. The `envs` option is passed to the `sandbox.runCode()` method.

```js
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const result = await sandbox.runCode('import os; print(os.environ.get("MY_VAR"))', {
  envs: { 
    MY_VAR: 'my_value',
  },
})
```

```python
sandbox = Sandbox.create()
result = sandbox.run_code(
    'import os; print(os.environ.get("MY_VAR"))',
    envs={
        'MY_VAR': 'my_value'
    }
)
```

--------------------------------

### Start Custom Sandbox with Template ID (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Starts a new sandbox instance using a previously built custom template. The template ID is passed to the `Sandbox.create` method in Python.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create(template='YOUR_TEMPLATE_ID')
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies for E2B and Claude SDKs

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/code-interpreting/analyze-data-with-ai/page.mdx

Installs the necessary E2B Code Interpreter and Anthropic SDKs. This is a prerequisite for interacting with the E2B Sandbox and the Claude LLM for AI-generated code execution.

```js
npm i @e2b/code-interpreter @anthropic-ai/sdk dotenv
```

```python
pip install e2b-code-interpreter anthropic python-dotenv
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/page.mdx

Installs the E2B command-line interface. This is the first step to manage sandbox templates and interact with the E2B platform.

```bash
brew install e2b
```

```bash
npm i -g @e2b/cli
```

--------------------------------

### Install s3fs for Amazon S3 in Dockerfile

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/connect-bucket/page.mdx

This Dockerfile installs the s3fs package, which enables mounting Amazon S3 buckets as a file system. It starts from a base Ubuntu image and uses apt-get to install the s3fs package.

```docker
FROM ubuntu:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install s3fs

```

--------------------------------

### Environment Variable Setup (.env)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/page.mdx

Example of how to store your E2B API key in a .env file for local development.

```bash
E2B_API_KEY=e2b_***
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B Python SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/packages/python-sdk/README.md

Installs the E2B Python SDK using pip. This is the first step to interact with E2B sandboxes from your Python applications. No specific inputs or outputs are detailed, but successful execution means the package is available for import.

```shell
pip install e2b-code-interpreter
```
